title: erigeron multifidus var. discoideus
intro: Erigeron multifidus var. discoideus, also known as Pale Fleabane, is a plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the northwestern United States and western Canada. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The medicinal properties of Pale Fleabane can provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, improving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Pale Fleabane can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or orally to harness its medicinal properties.
